questions.scenario: You have 0 Aetherflow stacks. Energy Drain is available.
questions.question: What should you do?
questions.explanation: Energy Drain grants 2 Aetherflow stacks and the Further Ruin buff. Use it when you have 0 stacks to get resources.

questions.scenario: You have 2 Aetherflow stacks. Energy Drain is coming off cooldown in 5 seconds.
questions.question: What should you do with your stacks?
questions.explanation: Don't let Energy Drain come off cooldown while holding stacks - you'd waste the new stacks. Spend your current stacks before refreshing.

questions.scenario: You have 1 Aetherflow stack. The boss is about to die.
questions.question: Should you use Fester/Necrotize?
questions.explanation: If the boss is dying, convert your stacks to damage with Fester/Necrotize. Unused stacks at fight end are wasted resources.

questions.scenario: Energy Drain has a 60 second cooldown.
questions.question: How should you plan Aetherflow usage?
options: Plan to empty stacks before each refresh
questions.explanation: Plan your Aetherflow spending around the 60s cooldown. Aim to spend both stacks before Energy Drain comes back up to avoid waste.

questions.scenario: Energy Drain also grants Further Ruin buff.
questions.question: What does Further Ruin enable?
questions.explanation: Further Ruin enables Ruin IV, a powerful instant-cast spell. Use it before the buff expires - it's free damage from Energy Drain.

questions.scenario: You summon Ifrit and receive 2 attunement stacks.
questions.question: What do attunement stacks represent?
options: Number of primal abilities to use
questions.explanation: Attunement stacks represent how many primal abilities you can use. Ifrit gives 2 (Ruby Rite), Titan/Garuda give 4 each.

questions.scenario: You're in Ifrit phase. Ruby Rite has a cast time.
questions.question: When is Ifrit phase best?
questions.explanation: Ifrit has the highest damage but requires casting. Save Ifrit for periods when you can stand still to get full value from Ruby Rite.

questions.scenario: You're expecting heavy movement in 10 seconds.
questions.question: Which primal should you summon?
questions.explanation: Titan's Topaz Rite is instant cast. Summon Titan before movement phases so you can deal damage while moving.

questions.scenario: You summon Garuda and get 4 Emerald attunement stacks.
questions.question: What's special about Garuda?
options: Fast instant casts but lower per-hit damage
questions.explanation: Garuda's Emerald Rite is fast and instant but lower damage per cast. Good for moderate movement and quick attunement spending.

questions.scenario: You must spend all primal attunement before:
questions.question: What's the requirement for Demi-Summons?
options: All 3 primals must be summoned and spent
questions.explanation: You must summon and spend all 3 primals (Ifrit, Titan, Garuda) before your next Demi-Summon becomes available.

questions.scenario: You're in Ifrit phase with Crimson Cyclone available.
questions.question: What does Crimson Cyclone do?
options: Gap closer GCD to the target
questions.explanation: Crimson Cyclone is a gap closer that moves you to the target. Follow it with Crimson Strike for a melee combo. Position yourself first.

questions.scenario: You just cast Topaz Rite (Titan phase).
questions.question: What oGCD becomes available?
questions.explanation: Mountain Buster procs after each Topaz Rite. Weave it immediately - it's an oGCD that adds significant damage during Titan phase.

questions.scenario: You're in Garuda phase. Slipstream is available.
questions.question: What's special about Slipstream?
questions.explanation: Slipstream is a channeled AoE that takes about 3 seconds. Use it when you can stand still - it's wasted if you move during the channel.

questions.scenario: You use Crimson Cyclone and are now in melee range.
questions.question: What should you follow with?
questions.explanation: Crimson Strike follows Crimson Cyclone and requires melee range. It's a powerful follow-up - don't skip it by moving away too soon.

questions.scenario: Favor abilities are gained after summoning the upgraded primal.
questions.question: When do you get Crimson Cyclone?
questions.explanation: Favor abilities like Crimson Cyclone unlock when you summon the upgraded version of a primal (Ifrit II/III, not the base egi).

questions.scenario: Demi-Summons last 15 seconds.
questions.question: What do you spam during Demi phases?
questions.explanation: During Demi phases, your GCD changes to the Demi-specific spell (Astral Impulse for Bahamut, Fountain of Fire for Phoenix, etc.).

questions.scenario: You summon Bahamut.
questions.question: What's Bahamut's main GCD?
questions.explanation: During Bahamut phase, spam Astral Impulse. It's your GCD during this Demi-Summon, and Bahamut attacks alongside you.

questions.scenario: You summon Phoenix.
questions.question: What extra benefit does Phoenix provide?
questions.explanation: Phoenix phase includes Rekindle, which heals a party member. Phoenix provides support alongside damage - useful during healing-intensive phases.

questions.scenario: You summon Solar Bahamut at level 100.
questions.question: What's the Demi-Summon rotation cycle?
questions.explanation: The cycle is: Bahamut → 3 Primals → Phoenix → 3 Primals → Solar Bahamut → repeat. Each Demi follows spending all primals.

questions.scenario: All Demi-Summon GCDs are instant cast.
questions.question: Why is this significant?
questions.explanation: Demi GCDs being instant means you have complete freedom of movement during Demi phases. Use these windows for mechanics.

questions.scenario: You're in Bahamut phase. Enkindle Bahamut is available.
questions.question: How many times can you use Enkindle per Demi phase?
questions.explanation: Enkindle can only be used once per Demi-Summon phase. It's a massive damage oGCD - don't forget to use it!

questions.scenario: Astral Flow changes based on your current Demi.
questions.question: What is Astral Flow during Bahamut?
questions.explanation: During Bahamut, Astral Flow becomes Deathflare - a powerful finisher. During Phoenix it's Rekindle (heal), during Solar Bahamut it's Sunflare.

questions.scenario: Searing Light is active (your party buff).
questions.question: What ability becomes available during Searing Light?
questions.explanation: During Searing Light window, you gain access to Searing Flash - an additional oGCD for extra burst damage.

questions.scenario: You're in Demi phase. When should you use Enkindle?
questions.question: What's the optimal Enkindle timing?
questions.explanation: Use Enkindle early in the Demi phase. If the boss becomes untargetable or the phase ends, you'd lose it. Don't risk missing your window.

questions.scenario: Phoenix phase is ending. You haven't used Rekindle.
questions.question: Should you use Rekindle before Phoenix leaves?
options: Yes - it's lost when Phoenix leaves
questions.explanation: Rekindle is only available during Phoenix phase. If you don't use it, you lose it. Cast it on the tank or anyone who might need healing.

questions.scenario: Searing Light is a 2-minute party buff.
questions.question: What does Searing Light provide?
questions.explanation: Searing Light gives the party 5% damage increase for 20 seconds. It's your raid buff - align it with other party cooldowns.

questions.scenario: Other raid buffs are being used at 0:00, 2:00, 4:00.
questions.question: When should you use Searing Light?
options: Aligned with party buffs at 2-min marks
questions.explanation: Align Searing Light with other party raid buffs for stacked multiplicative damage. 0:00, 2:00, 4:00 etc. are standard burst windows.

questions.scenario: You're 5 seconds away from a Demi-Summon.
questions.question: Should you wait to use Searing Light with your Demi?
options: Yes - Demi phases have high damage
questions.explanation: Align Searing Light with your Demi-Summon phases when possible. Demi phases are your highest damage - put them under your own buff.

questions.scenario: A Dragoon announces Battle Litany is coming.
questions.question: What should you do?
options: Coordinate your Searing Light to stack
questions.explanation: Coordinate with other raid buffs. If Battle Litany is coming in a few seconds, hold Searing Light to stack buffs for maximum party damage.

questions.scenario: Your Searing Light drifted and is now 15 seconds off from party buffs.
questions.question: What should you consider?
questions.explanation: If significantly out of sync, consider delaying your next Searing Light to realign with party burst windows. Stacked buffs > individual uptime.

questions.scenario: You need to optimize primal order for an upcoming fight.
questions.question: What's the recommended primal order?
questions.explanation: Titan → Garuda → Ifrit is optimal. Titan gives movement flexibility early, save Ifrit (casted) for when you can stand still.

questions.scenario: You're between primal/demi phases with nothing else to do.
questions.question: What's your filler GCD?
questions.explanation: Ruin III is your filler spell between primal and Demi phases. Always be casting - never stand idle waiting.

questions.scenario: You have the Further Ruin buff (Ruin IV ready).
questions.question: When should you use Ruin IV?
options: Before it expires or Energy Drain refreshes
questions.explanation: Use Ruin IV before the buff expires. Also spend it before Energy Drain comes back up, or the new Further Ruin buff would be wasted.

questions.scenario: There are 4 enemies grouped together.
questions.question: What changes in AoE?
options: Use Tri-disaster filler, primal Catastrophe variants
questions.explanation: In AoE, use Tri-disaster instead of Ruin III, and primal Catastrophe variants (Ruby/Topaz/Emerald Catastrophe) instead of Rite spells.

questions.scenario: Why is Titan first generally recommended?
questions.question: What's the advantage of Titan first?
questions.explanation: Starting with Titan (instant casts) gives you movement flexibility early. Save Ifrit for when mechanics allow standing still to cast.
